source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple sessions in same cwd
notes: Two concurrent codex sessions launched in the SAME cwd (driver start_session step; the 2nd shares session 1's directory, so codex's per-directory trust cache suppresses a second trust dialog). Each codex process writes its OWN rollout under ~/.codex/sessions and the daemon binds each session to the PID holding that rollout open (PID-cwd disambiguation). Every phase anchors on a WORKING transition, never a ready: codex's process scanner emits a pre-session proc-<PID> row that is born in ready BEFORE the rollout file is discovered and never enters working, so a ready-anchored new_session phase would greedily bind to that placeholder. Anchoring on working skips both the placeholder (never works) and any already-matched session. codex mints its own rollout UUID (session_id_assignment:false), so the two distinct session_ids are asserted via new_session/same_session_as relations, not literal ids. Assertions: (1) session 1 does an independent working→ready turn; (2) a DISTINCT session (s2_turn1_start carries new_session:true) does its own working→ready turn; (3) session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is alive (s1_turn2_*, pinned same_session_as s1_turn1_start), proving its PID-to-session binding stayed stable and never flapped to session 2. Phase order follows the recipe: s1 turn → start_session → s2 turn → s1 turn again.

text: Session 1 first prompt drives ready → working (anchors session 1's rollout id; the pre-session proc-<PID> row and the rollout's born-ready transition are both skipped by anchoring on working)
Session 1 first turn completes: working → ready

Session 2 appears in the SAME cwd as a DISTINCT rollout id and drives its first turn ready → working — independent of session 1 (new_session proves it is not session 1; anchoring on working skips codex's born-ready proc-<PID> placeholder)
Session 2 first turn completes: working → ready
Session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is still alive — a working transition on session 1's original rollout id proves the binding stayed stable and did not flap to session 2
Session 1 second turn completes: working → ready
